verses.text: Here are the families of Noah's three sons after the flood — Shem, Ham, and Japheth. From them came all the nations of the earth. The chapter that follows is a map of humanity.

Japheth's line spreads northward and westward. His descendants populate the coastlands and islands — these are the peoples Greek geography later knew as the European world. Each son becomes the founder of a nation.

Japheth's line spreads northward and westward. His descendants populate the coastlands and islands — these are the peoples Greek geography later knew as the European world. Each son becomes the founder of a nation.

Japheth's line spreads northward and westward. His descendants populate the coastlands and islands — these are the peoples Greek geography later knew as the European world. Each son becomes the founder of a nation.

Japheth's line spreads northward and westward. His descendants populate the coastlands and islands — these are the peoples Greek geography later knew as the European world. Each son becomes the founder of a nation.
Ham's sons spread south and east — toward Egypt, Africa, Arabia. Cush is Ethiopia; Mizraim is Egypt; Canaan settles the land Israel will one day enter.
Ham's sons spread south and east — toward Egypt, Africa, Arabia. Cush is Ethiopia; Mizraim is Egypt; Canaan settles the land Israel will one day enter.
Cush also fathers Nimrod, the first mighty hunter on the earth. The word mighty here suggests a warrior-king, not just a sportsman. His skill becomes a proverb: like Nimrod the mighty hunter.
Cush also fathers Nimrod, the first mighty hunter on the earth. The word mighty here suggests a warrior-king, not just a sportsman. His skill becomes a proverb: like Nimrod the mighty hunter.

Nimrod founds an empire — Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh in the land of Shinar, then Nineveh and other cities. These names will reappear later in Scripture. The first great empire-builder rises in the east.

Nimrod founds an empire — Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh in the land of Shinar, then Nineveh and other cities. These names will reappear later in Scripture. The first great empire-builder rises in the east.

Nimrod founds an empire — Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh in the land of Shinar, then Nineveh and other cities. These names will reappear later in Scripture. The first great empire-builder rises in the east.
Mizraim — Egypt — fathers the peoples around the Nile, including the Philistines who would later be Israel's neighbors. Geography is shaping the future of God's story.
Mizraim — Egypt — fathers the peoples around the Nile, including the Philistines who would later be Israel's neighbors. Geography is shaping the future of God's story.

Canaan's line spreads through the lands Israel will later enter — Sidon, the Hittites, the Jebusites, the Amorites. The names that fill the conquest narratives all begin here, with one of Noah's grandsons.

Canaan's line spreads through the lands Israel will later enter — Sidon, the Hittites, the Jebusites, the Amorites. The names that fill the conquest narratives all begin here, with one of Noah's grandsons.

Canaan's line spreads through the lands Israel will later enter — Sidon, the Hittites, the Jebusites, the Amorites. The names that fill the conquest narratives all begin here, with one of Noah's grandsons.

Canaan's line spreads through the lands Israel will later enter — Sidon, the Hittites, the Jebusites, the Amorites. The names that fill the conquest narratives all begin here, with one of Noah's grandsons.
The Canaanite border stretched from Sidon to Gaza, all the way to Sodom and Gomorrah. These two cities are mentioned by name even now — the reader is being prepared for chapter 19.
The Canaanite border stretched from Sidon to Gaza, all the way to Sodom and Gomorrah. These two cities are mentioned by name even now — the reader is being prepared for chapter 19.
Shem's line is the most important for the rest of Genesis — through it the Messiah will come. He is called the father of all the children of Eber, ancestor of the Hebrews.
Shem's line is the most important for the rest of Genesis — through it the Messiah will come. He is called the father of all the children of Eber, ancestor of the Hebrews.
Five generations are listed. One son of Eber is named Peleg — meaning division — because in his days the earth was divided. The Tower of Babel in chapter 11 is being foreshadowed.
Five generations are listed. One son of Eber is named Peleg — meaning division — because in his days the earth was divided. The Tower of Babel in chapter 11 is being foreshadowed.
Five generations are listed. One son of Eber is named Peleg — meaning division — because in his days the earth was divided. The Tower of Babel in chapter 11 is being foreshadowed.
Joktan, Peleg's brother, fathers thirteen sons whose names locate them in southern Arabia. The Semitic family tree is spreading wide.
Joktan, Peleg's brother, fathers thirteen sons whose names locate them in southern Arabia. The Semitic family tree is spreading wide.
Joktan, Peleg's brother, fathers thirteen sons whose names locate them in southern Arabia. The Semitic family tree is spreading wide.
Joktan, Peleg's brother, fathers thirteen sons whose names locate them in southern Arabia. The Semitic family tree is spreading wide.
Their territory stretches from Mesha to Sephar. These are real lands, real boundaries — Genesis is grounding itself in geography all the way through.
Their territory stretches from Mesha to Sephar. These are real lands, real boundaries — Genesis is grounding itself in geography all the way through.
Seventy nations in total come from Noah's three sons. Seventy is a number of completeness — the whole human family is accounted for, before Babel scatters them.
